title: Patterns with even and odd
description: Use an understanding of even and odd numbers to figure out number patterns.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following have an even sum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.
[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

$\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $+$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number  $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 


[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $+$  $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $=$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $+$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number  $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

The following have even sums:

 - $\blueD2 + \blueD{12}$  
 -  $\maroonD{5\text{ cows}} + \maroonD{3\text{ cows}}$

choices.content: ![A group of 5 cows. The group has a rectangle around it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/049f66255b8f81b1ac09c999329985a416e6d1b4)$+$![A group of 3 cows. The group has a rectangle around it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e38beb3c440874af8fd2ce930b234f33eb73049a)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the sum of an even number plus an even number?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.
[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

$\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $+$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number  $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 


[[☃ image 1]]
The sum of an even number plus an even number is **always even**.

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $+$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number  $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]
The sum of an odd number plus an odd number is **always even**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Luis adds two numbers and gets an even sum.  

**What could be true about the numbers Luis added?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

$\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $+$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number  $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 


[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $+$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number  $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 


[[☃ image 1]]

The following are true about Luis numbers:

- Both numbers were even
- Both numbers were odd

choices.content: One number was even and one was odd

assessment_items.question_content:
Sara adds two numbers and gets an odd sum.  

**What could be true about the numbers Sara added?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.
[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

$\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $+$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $=$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

The following is true about Sara numbers:

- One number was even and one was odd

choices.content: One number was even and one was odd

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the product of an even number times an even number?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

$\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $\times$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number  $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

When we multiply by an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number, we make an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number of groups.  

So, the product can be split into $2$ equal groups.

The product will always be $\blueD{\text{even}}$.
The product of an even number times an even number is **always even**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the product of an odd number times an even number?**

hints:

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $\times$  $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

When we multiply by an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number, we make an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number of groups.  

So, the product can be split into $2$ equal groups.

The product will always be $\blueD{\text{even}}$.
The product of an odd number times an even number is **always even**.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.
[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $\times$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number  $=$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

When we multiply by an $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number, we make an $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number of groups.  

So, the product **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups.

The product will always be $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$.
The product of an odd number times an odd number is **always odd**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Liam multiplies two numbers and gets an even product.  

**What could be true about the numbers Liam multiplied?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.
[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

$\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $\times$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number  $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

When we multiply by an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number, we make an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number of groups.  

So, the product can be split into $2$ equal groups.

The product will always be $\blueD{\text{even}}$.

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $\times$  $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

When we multiply by an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number, we make an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number of groups.  

So, the product can be split into $2$ equal groups.

The product will always be $\blueD{\text{even}}$.

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $\times$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number  $=$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number 


[[☃ image 1]]

When we multiply by an $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number, we make an $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number of groups.  

So, the product **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups.

The product will always be $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$.

The following are true about Liam's numbers:

- Both numbers were even
- One number was even and one was odd

choices.content: One number was even and one was odd

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of following have an odd product?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

###$\maroonD3$ $\maroonD{\text{fives}}$


[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD3\times\maroonD5 = \maroonD{15}$

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $\times$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number  $=$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

When we multiply by an $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number, we make an $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number of groups.  

So, the product **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups.

The product will always be $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$.

###$\blueD4\times\maroonD7$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD4\times\maroonD7 = \blueD{28}$

The following have an odd product:  

-  $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $\times$ $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number
- $\maroonD3$ $\maroonD{\text{fives}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of following have an even product?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.
[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

###$\maroonD5 \times \maroonD3$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD5 \times \maroonD3 = \maroonD{15}$

$\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $\times$  $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

When we multiply by an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number, we make an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number of groups.  

So, the product can be split into $2$ equal groups.

The product will always be $\blueD{\text{even}}$.

$\blueD8\maroonD{\text{ fives}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD8\times\maroonD5 = \blueD{40}$

The following have an even product:  

-  $\blueD8\maroonD{\text{ fives}}$
-  $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number $\times$  $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of following have an even product?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number is a multiple of $2$. It can be split into $2$ equal groups.
[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$, $\blueD4$, $\blueD6$, $\blueD8$, $\blueD{10}$,  $. .  .$

An $\maroonD{\text{odd}}$ number is **not** a multiple of $2$. It **cannot** be split into $2$ equal groups. 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD1$, $\maroonD3$, $\maroonD5$, $\maroonD7$, $\maroonD9$, $. . . $

$\maroonD{5\text{ fives}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD5\times\maroonD5=\maroonD{25}$

$\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $\times$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number  $=$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number 

[[☃ image 1]]

When we multiply by an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number, we make an $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number of groups.  

So, the product can be split into $2$ equal groups.

The product will always be $\blueD{\text{even}}$.

###$\blueD4\times\maroonD3$


[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD4\times\maroonD3 = \blueD{12}$

The following have an even product:  

-  $\blueD4\times\maroonD3$
-  $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number $\times$ $\blueD{\text{even}}$ number
